title: Optional chaining should be avoided if returning undefined throws an error
description: Optional chaining should not be used if returning "undefined" throws an errorAnalyze your codeintentionality-completereliabilityBugbad-practicees2015Why is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoThe optional chaining operator?.allows to access a deeply nested property, returningundefinedif the property or any intermediate object isundefined.This usually means that the expression is expected to evaluate asundefinedin some cases. Therefore, using the optional chaining operator in a context where returningundefinedthrows an error can lead to runtime exceptions.(event?.callback)(); // Noncompliant: when 'event' does not have 'callback' property TypeError is thrown const { code } = event?.error; // Noncompliant: when 'event' does not have 'error' property TypeError is thrown func(...event?.val
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Avoid calling functions from optional chaining - may throw TypeError if undefined
Avoid calling methods from optional chaining - may throw TypeError if undefined
Avoid destructuring from optional chaining - may throw TypeError if undefined
Avoid spread operator with optional chaining - may throw TypeError if undefined
Avoid spread operator with optional chaining - may throw TypeError if undefined
Avoid passing optional chaining function calls as parameters - may throw TypeError
